title: aloysia gratissima var. sellowii
intro: Aloysia gratissima var. sellowii, also known as Brazilian lemon verbena, is a tropical plant species within the Verbenaceae family, native to Brazil and parts of South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves and essential oil have been found to have health benefits such as reducing stress, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Brazilian lemon verbena is commonly prepared as a tea or infused oil, and its essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
